title: centaurea pseudodepressa
intro: Centaurea pseudodepressa is a flowering plant species native to the Balkan Peninsula, classified within the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea pseudodepressa include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in wound healing and antimicrobial applications. Herbal preparations of Centaurea pseudodepressa can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to take advantage of the plant's medicinal properties.
